user_message: I have this meeting transcript from Humongous Insurance that needs investigate the decline. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 13:34 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with competitive positioning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 08:03 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 11:25 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-N trap (Opportunity creation requires approval ceremony when CSA can self-validate from ); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-N failure mode (Opportunity creation requires approval ceremony when CSA can self-validate from )
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-N -- Opportunity creation requires approval ceremony when CSA can self-validate from
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
